title: [Bug]: NatsEnvelope preserves empty normalized header names
summary: NatsEnvelope header normalization currently preserves header names that normalize to an empty string.
observed: Creating an envelope with a blank or whitespace-only header name keeps that header in envelope.headers.
expected: The envelope should drop empty header names at the trust boundary while preserving valid headers.
reproduction: Create NatsEnvelope(headers={'   ': 'value'}) and observe that the normalized headers still include a blank key.
failing_test: Add a unit test that verifies whitespace-only header names are removed from normalized envelope headers.
impact: Empty header names are not useful for routing or storage and can make persisted metadata harder to audit.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. This only affects malformed header metadata normalization.
security: The fix should drop invalid header keys without logging header values.
acceptance: Whitespace-only header names are not present in normalized envelope headers.
Valid headers remain preserved exactly enough for case-insensitive lookup.
The focused regression test passes after the fix.
tests: Run focused envelope tests and full validation.
documentation: Update release notes and test report.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
